blocks:
Ayahs

يسٓ (۱)
Ya Seen! (1)
وَٱلْقُرْءَانِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (۲)
By the Wise Quran, (2)
إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۳)
you are indeed one of the apostles, (3)
عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (٤)
on a straight path. (4)

Key Points

According to narrations, Yā Sīn is the blessed name of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله.(589) The word Ḥakīm (Wise) can mean both "possessing wisdom" (dhul-ḥikmah) and "firm/unshakeable" (muḥkam), similar to the first verse of Sūrah Hūd where He says: «أُحْكِمَتْ آيَاتُهُ» (Its verses have been made firm), meaning the verses of the Quran are firm. The Straight Path is not a path where we only reach the destination after traversing its entirety; rather, to whatever extent we travel, we have reached a portion of the destination. This is like the acquisition of knowledge: the further we advance, the greater the amount of knowledge we have attained. In response to the various accusations leveled against the Prophet—that he was a poet, a soothsayer, a sorcerer, or a madman—Allah, the Wise, emphasizes the Prophet's mission by swearing by the wisdom-filled Quran.

589) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Falsehood has no way into the Quran, and all of it is firm and stable. «وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ»

2- Swearing by the Quran, especially by Allah, indicates its greatness and sanctity. «وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ»

3- In the face of an onslaught of accusations and unjust attempts at weakening, correct and comprehensive support is necessary. «إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ»

4- Men of God are not alone throughout history; they stand alongside other Divine Messengers. «لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ»

5- The path of the Prophet is the path of Allah. «إِنَّ رَبِّي عَلَى صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ»(590), «إِنَّكَ... عَلَى صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ» (The Straight Path is the path of Allah and His Messenger.)

6- Three things are necessary for success: A: A codified program (the Wise Quran). B: An aware executor and leader («لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ»). C: A clear path («صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ»).

7- The foundation of belief must be firm. (The Wise God, the Wise Quran, and the Prophet, the teacher of wisdom). «يُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ» 590) Hūd, 56.

Explanations

Portrait of Sūrah Yā Sīn

This Sūrah was revealed in Mecca, has eighty-three verses, and is named after its first verse, which consists of disjointed letters (al-ḥurūf al-muqaṭṭa‘ah). The contents of this Sūrah revolve around matters of belief, and it is recommended to teach it to children and to dedicate the reward of its recitation to the deceased. This Sūrah begins with Allah's testimony to the mission of the Prophet of Islam and continues with the account of the mission of three Divine Prophets. In one part of this Sūrah, some signs of the Lord's greatness in existence are mentioned as proofs of Monotheism (Tawḥīd), and in another part, issues related to the Resurrection (Ma‘ād), questioning and answering in the Court of Judgment, and the characteristics of Paradise and Hell are discussed. In narrations, Sūrah Yā Sīn is referred to as the "Heart of the Quran."(588)

588) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

تَنزِيلَ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ (٥)
[It is a scripture] sent down gradually from the All-mighty, the All-merciful (5)
لِتُنذِرَ قَوْمًا مَّآ أُنذِرَ ءَابَآؤُهُمْ فَهُمْ غَـٰفِلُونَ (٦)
that you may warn a people whose fathers were not warned, so they are oblivious. (6)

Messages

1- Allah is Mighty towards His opponents and Merciful towards His allies. «العزیز الرحیم»

2- The source of the revelation of the Quran is Power and Mercy, and whoever adheres to it attains might and mercy. «تنزیل العزیز الرحیم»

3- Admonition (warning) is the goal of the prophets' mission. «لتنذر»

4- Warning is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «ما انذر آباءهم» (Even though people may still remain heedless despite it.)

5- The basis and foundation of propagation and warning must be the teachings of the Quran. «تنزیل العزیز الرحیم لتنذر»

6- Even the warnings of the Quran arise from Allah’s Mercy and compassion. «الرحیم لتنذر»

Ayahs

لَقَدْ حَقَّ ٱلْقَوْلُ عَلَىٰٓ أَكْثَرِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۷)
The word has certainly become due against most of them, so they will not have faith. (7)
إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا فِىٓ أَعْنَـٰقِهِمْ أَغْلَـٰلًا فَهِىَ إِلَى ٱلْأَذْقَانِ فَهُم مُّقْمَحُونَ (۸)
Indeed We have put iron collars around their necks, which are up to the chins, so their heads are upturned. (8)

Key Points

Idhqān (اذقان) is the plural of dhaqn (ذقن), meaning 'chin,' and muqmahūn (مقمحون) is derived from qamḥ al-ba'īr (قمح البعیر), meaning the camel raising its head. This means that just as water is offered to the camel but it raises its head, the truth is presented to these unworthy people, but they raise their heads and do not bow down before the truth. The intent behind placing shackles (ghull) on their necks is either their punishment in the Hereafter, similar to the Ayah: «اذ الاغلال فى اعناقهم» (591), or it refers to the existence of superstitious beliefs that are like heavy shackles on their necks, similar to Ayah 157 of Surah Al-A'raf, which states: «یضع عنهم اصرهم و الاغلال الّتى كانت علیهم» —where removing the superstitions that have bound them like heavy shackles is among the duties of the Prophets. Indeed, the Prophets want to remove the shackles, but the people themselves do not want this. The intent of al-Qawl (القول), based on the context of Ayah 13 of Surah As-Sajdah, where He said: «و لكن حقّ القول منّى لاملئنّ جهنّم من الجنّة و النّاس اجمعین», is Allah's command regarding the punishment of the followers of Satan.

591) Ghafir, 71.

Messages

1- Most heedless people do not incline toward the truth. «فهم غافلون لقد حقّ القول على اكثرهم»

2- The opposition of the majority should not discourage the preacher and the leader. «اكثرهم... لایؤمنون»

3- Heedlessness is the ground for disbelief (kufr). «فهم غافلون - فهم لا یؤمنون»

4- Although Allah places shackles on the necks of the disbelievers, they themselves have prepared the grounds for it. «لا یؤمنون انّا جعلنا فى اعناقهم اغلالاً»

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَا مِنۢ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ سَدًّا وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ سَدًّا فَأَغْشَيْنَـٰهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ (۹)
And We have put a barrier before them and a barrier behind them, then We have blind-folded them, so they do not see. (9)

Key Points

Although the Quran is firm and wisdom-teaching, the mission of the Prophet is certain, the path of religion is straight, and Allah is Strong and Merciful, yet because most people are heedless, faithless, and entangled in superstitions, wherever they turn, they encounter a strong barrier and a dead end.

The portrait of obstinate individuals is drawn very precisely and interestingly in this Ayah: Firstly: They are heedless, «فهم غافلون» (so they are heedless), and consequently, they do not believe. «فهم لا یؤمنون» (so they will not believe). Secondly: Superstitions have entangled them. «فى اعناقهم اغلالا» (in their necks are chains), and like a stubborn camel, they refuse to accept the truth. «فهم مقمحون» (so they are stiff-necked). They are besieged from the front and the back, «سدّا» (a barrier), and a curtain has been drawn over their eyes, so they do not see. Perhaps the meaning of «من بین ایدیهم سدّاً» (a barrier before them) is man's long-term hopes for the future, and the meaning of «من خلفهم سدّاً» (a barrier behind them) is heedlessness regarding past transgressions, as those hopes and that heedlessness are two major obstacles to seeing the truth. In these verses, two punishments are set against two major deviations: «فهم غافلون - فهم لا یؤمنون» (so they are heedless - so they will not believe), «جعلنا فى اعناقهم اغلالاً - جعلنا من بین ایدیهم سدّا» (We have put chains on their necks - We have put a barrier before them).

Messages

1- He has hundreds of lights yet walks astray; let him fall and see his own recompense. «و جعلنا من بین ایدیهم سدّا»

2- The heedless faithless neither take lessons from the past of the disbelievers, «خلفهم» (behind them), nor from the miracle and argument that is before them. «من بین ایدیهم» (before them).

3- The disbeliever is at a dead end. «سدّا» (a barrier).

4- In propagation (Da'wah), we should liken abstract concepts to tangible things. «سدّا» (a barrier).

Ayahs

وَسَوَآءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَأَنذَرْتَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تُنذِرْهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٠)
It is the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not have faith. (10)
إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَخَشِىَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۖ فَبَشِّرْهُ بِمَغْفِرَةٍ وَأَجْرٍ كَرِيمٍ (۱۱)
You can only warn someone who follows the Reminder and fears the All-beneficent in secret; so give him the good news of forgiveness and a noble reward. (11)

Key Points

The meaning of al-Dhikr (الذّكر), considering other verses, is the Quran. As we read in Ayah 9 of Surah Al-Hijr: «انّا نحن نزّلنا الذّكر و انّا له لحافظون» (Indeed, We have sent down the Dhikr, and indeed, We will be its guardian.) A group of disbelievers (kuffār) and polytheists (mushrikūn) are never amenable to guidance, and the Prophet's warning regarding them is ineffective. Regarding the disbelievers: «انّ الّذین كفروا سواء علیهم ء انذرتهم ام لم تنذرهم لا یؤمنون» (Indeed, those who disbelieve—it is all the same for them whether you warn them or do not warn them—they will not believe.) (592) And regarding the polytheists: «سواء علیكم ادعوتموهم ام انتم صامتون» (It is the same for you whether you call upon them or you remain silent.) (593) The meaning of khashī ar-Raḥmān bi-l-Ghayb (خشى الرّحمن بالغیب) is either fear of God internally, or fear of God in places hidden from people's eyes, or fear of Allah concerning the Court of Resurrection, which is among the unseen matters (ghaybī). Whenever a person stands before a great personality, their awe and majesty cause a state to arise in the person, which is called khashyah (reverential fear). This is different from the state of khawf (ordinary fear) and dread that arises from punishment and torment.

(592) Al-Baqarah, 6. (593) Al-A'rāf, 193.

Messages

1- What good is preaching to the dark-hearted? An iron nail does not penetrate stone. «سواء علیهم...»

2- The matter of completing the proof (itmām al-ḥujjah) is separate from the matter of the speech's effectiveness and people's receptivity. (Even though there is no likelihood of effect regarding a group, the Prophet must complete the proof against them and warn them.) «سواء علیهم...»

3- The Quran is the source of dhikr (remembrance), and following it is the means of recalling forgotten things and awakening the dormant human nature (fiṭrah). «اتبّع الذّكر»

4- Allah's vast Mercy should not lead to our recklessness. «خشى الرّحمن»

5- The sign of true faith and khashyah is piety in secret. «بالغیب»

6- Fear and hope are necessary side by side. In the phrase «خشى الرّحمن», there is both khashyah (reverential fear) and Raḥmah (Mercy).

7- Fear of God in solitude and internally is important, not outwardly and in the presence of people. «خشى الرّحمن بالغیب»

8- The duty of the Prophets is both warning and caution, «انّما تنذر...», and hope and glad tidings. «فبشّره»

9- The one who receives the glad tidings of Paradise is the one whom the Prophets' warning has caused to remember. «تنذر من اتبّع الذّكر... فبشّره...»

Ayah

إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَنَكْتُبُ مَا قَدَّمُوا۟ وَءَاثَـٰرَهُمْ ۚ وَكُلَّ شَىْءٍ أَحْصَيْنَـٰهُ فِىٓ إِمَامٍ مُّبِينٍ (۱۲)
Indeed it is We who revive the dead and write what they have sent ahead and their effects [which they left behind], and We have figured everything in a manifest Imam. (12)

Key Points

Whatever issues forth from man is recorded in his file of deeds and will be presented to him in the form of a book on the Day of Judgment. These books are of three types: A: The personal book. «اقرء كتابك» (Read your book) (594) B: The book of nations. «كلّ اُمّة تدعى الى كتابها» (Every nation is called to its book) (595) C: The comprehensive book. «كلّ شى ء احصیناه فى امام مبین» (We have enumerated everything in a manifest Imam/Register), «فى لوح محفوظ» (in a Preserved Tablet) (596)

The word Imam (leader/register) has been applied both to a book, «و من قبله كتاب موسى اماما» (and before it was the Book of Moses as a guide) (597), and to individuals, «انّى جاعلك للنّاس اماما» (Indeed, I will make you an Imam for mankind) (598). In narrations, Hadrat Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام has been introduced as one of the manifestations of the «امام مبین» (Manifest Imam/Register), possessing all knowledge. (599) Most commentators (Mufassirun) consider the meaning of «امام مبین» (Manifest Imam/Register) to be the Preserved Tablet (Lawh Mahfuz), which is referred to in the Quran as «اُمّ الكتاب» (The Mother of the Book) (600). Since this book contains all knowledge, and everything and everyone follows the decrees written therein, it has been called the Imam (Guide/Register). (601)

594) Isra, 14. 595) Jathiyah, 28. 596) Buruj, 22. 597) Hud, 17. 598) Baqarah, 124. 599) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 600) Zukhruf, 4. 601) Tafsir Rahnuma.

Messages

1- Speak with clarity and decisiveness against the deviant and denial-based beliefs of the disbelievers. «انّا نحن نُحى» (Indeed, We give life)

2- All the dead will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment. «نحىِ الموتى» (We give life to the dead)

3- Man's file of deeds remains open, even after his death, until the Day of Judgment. «نكتب... و آثارهم» (We record... and their traces)

4- Not only deeds, but all their consequences (traces) are also recorded and taken into account in the reckoning of the Day of Judgment. «نكتب ما قدّموا آثارهم» (We record what they have put forth and their traces) (Whether good consequences, such as endowments (waqf), ongoing charity (sadaqah jariyah), teaching knowledge to people, etc., or evil consequences, such as establishing centers of immorality.)

5- Our dealings on the Day of Judgment are with Allah. «انّا، نحن، نحى، نكتب، احصینا» (Indeed We, We give life, We record, We have enumerated)

6- Man is not only responsible for his own deeds but also for their consequences. «و آثارهم» (and their traces)

7- Allah’s system of reckoning is precise. «كلّ شى ء احصیناه» (We have enumerated everything)

8- The Divine Court is manifest before everyone. «احصیناه فى امام مبین» (We have enumerated it in a manifest Imam/Register)

Ayah

وَٱضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلًا أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ إِذْ جَآءَهَا ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ (۱۳)
Cite for them the example of the inhabitants of the town when the apostles came to it. (13)

Key Points

From this Ayah up to seventeen other Ayahs, the history of a number of Prophets is narrated, who were commissioned to guide the people of their region. In the commentaries (Tafasir), it is stated that the village (Qaryah) referred to in this Ayah is the region of Antioch (Antakya), which was one of the ancient cities of Rome and is currently part of Turkish territory and one of its commercial cities. It is understood from the totality of these eighteen Ayahs that the people of this city were idolaters, and the Prophets had come to invite them to Monotheism (Tawhid) and to combat polytheism (Shirk).

Messages

1- The history of those who passed away is the best lesson for future generations. «واضرب لهم»

2- The educator and preacher must be familiar with history. «واضرب لهم»

3- Fixed principles and laws govern society, based on which the destiny of individuals and communities can be determined. Yes, the Divine traditions (Sunan) are constant and do not change regardless of differences in nations, individuals, time, or place. «واضرب لهم مثلاً»

4- The best examples are real and tangible ones, not imaginary ones. «واضرب لهم مثلاً»

5- When narrating a story, the lessons (Ibarat) are important; the name of the village, the race, the language, or the number of people is not important. «اصحاب القریة»

6- The Prophets went to the people; they did not wait for the people to come to them. «جاءها المرسلون»

Ayahs

إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَيْهِمُ ٱثْنَيْنِ فَكَذَّبُوهُمَا فَعَزَّزْنَا بِثَالِثٍ فَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ إِلَيْكُم مُّرْسَلُونَ (۱٤)
When We sent to them two [apostles], they impugned both of them. Then We reinforced them with a third, and they said, ‘We have indeed been sent to you.’ (14)
قَالُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُنَا وَمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ مِن شَىْءٍ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا تَكْذِبُونَ (۱٥)
They said, ‘You are no other than human beings like us, and the All-beneficent has not sent down anything, and you are only lying.’ (15)
قَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا يَعْلَمُ إِنَّآ إِلَيْكُمْ لَمُرْسَلُونَ (۱٦)
They said, ‘Our Lord knows that we have indeed been sent to you, (16)
وَمَا عَلَيْنَآ إِلَّا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱۷)
and our duty is only to communicate in clear terms.’ (17)

Key Points

Some people believe in the existence of Allah, but they do not accept the issue of prophethood and say that Allah has given us intellect and we have no need for revelation. This belief arises from an incorrect understanding of Allah, just as He states in another Ayah: «و ما قدروا اللّه حقّ قدره اذ قالوا ما انزل اللّه على بشر من شى ء»(602)

(602) Al-An'am, 91.

Messages

1- Sometimes, the call to truth and enjoining the good must be carried out collectively (as a group). «ارسلنا... اثنین فعزّزنا بثالث»

2- Obstinacy is an incurable ailment. (They even denied the prophets.) «كذبوهما»

3- Do not abandon your work or goal due to the enemy's denial; strengthen your ranks. «فكذّبوهما فعزّزنا بثالث»

4- Sometimes, several prophets were sent simultaneously to one nation. «اثنین... بثالث»

5- The number of preachers depends on the needs of the society. «ارسلنا الیهم اثنین فعزّزنا بثالث»

6- In management, when you send someone on a mission, do not abandon him; support and strengthen him. «فعزّزنا بثالث»

7- The abundance of qualified individuals leads to honor. «فعزّزنا بثالث»

8- Honor and disgrace are in the hands of Allah, even if an individual or a thing acts as an intermediary. «فعزّزنا»

9- In management, always maintain reserve forces. «بثالث»

10- The disbelievers ignore all perfections and view everything with a superficial outlook. «بشر مثلنا»

11- Some consider religious obligations, commands, and prohibitions to be contrary to Divine Mercy, imagining that mercy means unconditional freedom and the abandonment of man. «ما انزل الرّحمن من شى ء»

12- In the face of the enemy's continuous denials, calm your heart with the remembrance of Allah. «ربّنا یعلم...»

13- The duty of the prophets is enlightenment and informative propagation; they are not guarantors of the outcome. «و ما علینا الاّ البلاغ»

Ayahs

قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا تَطَيَّرْنَا بِكُمْ ۖ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهُوا۟ لَنَرْجُمَنَّكُمْ وَلَيَمَسَّنَّكُم مِّنَّا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱۸)
They said, ‘Indeed we take you for a bad omen. If you do not desist we will stone you, and surely a painful punishment will visit you from us.’ (18)
قَالُوا۟ طَـٰٓئِرُكُم مَّعَكُمْ ۚ أَئِن ذُكِّرْتُم ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ مُّسْرِفُونَ (۱۹)
They said, ‘Your bad omens attend you. What! If you are admonished.... Indeed, you are an unrestrained lot.’ (19)

Key Points

Taking a bad omen (pessimistic divination) is one of the superstitions that existed in the past and is still present today in the East and the West, and every region and nation takes a bad omen from something. Islam considers it shirk (polytheism) and has invalidated it with the phrase, «الطیرة شرك»(603) (Pessimistic divination is polytheism), and considers its expiation to be reliance upon Allah. «كفّارة الطیرة التوكّل»(604) (The expiation for pessimistic divination is reliance [on God]). Taking a bad omen has ominous effects, including: suspicion of people, stagnation in affairs, suggestion of failure, and feelings of inferiority. The worst of it is taking a bad omen regarding sacred things and the Divine saints.

603) Bihar, Vol. 55, p. 322. 604) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 198.

Messages

1- A clear response must be presented against every deviant statement. «قالوا انّا تطیّرنا بكم، قالوا طائركم معكم»

2- Denial «فكذّبوهما», humiliation «تطیّرنا بكم», and threat «لنرجمنّكم» are among the weapons of the disbelievers.

3- Divination and taking omens are customs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). «تطیّرنا بكم»

4- Whoever lacks logic resorts to superstitions. «تطیّرنا بكم»

5- Intellectual deviation is the prelude to practical deviation. «تطیّرنا بكم... لنرجمنّكم»

6- Disbelief leads to hard-heartedness and audacity toward the prophets. «لنرجمنّكم»

7- Disbelievers, who have no logic against the reasoning and method of the prophets, resort to threats after denial. «لنرجمنّكم و...»

8- The task of the prophets is to remove heedlessness and draw people's attention to their sound nature (fitrah). «ذكّرتم»

9- One manifestation of extravagance (israf) is rebellion and transgression (tughyan). «بل انتم قوم مسرفون»

10- The root of misfortunes is transgression and extravagance, not faith in Allah and the prophets. «بل انتم قوم مسرفون»

Ayahs

وَجَآءَ مِنْ أَقْصَا ٱلْمَدِينَةِ رَجُلٌ يَسْعَىٰ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱتَّبِعُوا۟ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۲٠)
There came a man hurrying from the city outskirts. He said, ‘O my people! Follow the apostles! (20)
ٱتَّبِعُوا۟ مَن لَّا يَسْـَٔلُكُمْ أَجْرًا وَهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ (۲۱)
Follow them who do not ask you any reward and they are rightly guided. (21)

Messages

1- Being far from the city is not a sign of being far from culture. «و جاءَ من أقصا المدینه رجل»

2- Supporting the truth (Haqq) has no borders or regions. «جاء من أقصا المدینه رجل»

3- Sometimes one must move and cry out alone. «جاء من أقصا المدینه رجل» Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Do not fear the scarcity of followers on the path of truth (Haqq).(605)

4- For the support of truth (Haqq), a long distance should not be an obstacle. «أقصا المدینة»

5- Advocating for the truth (Haqq) requires motivation and fervor, not a badge or title. «رجلٌ»

6- Supporting the truth (Haqq) must be accompanied by intensity, speed, logic, and emotion. «یسعى - یا قوم - هم مهتدون»

7- Breaking silence and crying out in environments of deviation, in support of righteous leadership, is praised by Allah. «قال یا قوم»

8- Sincerity in propagation is a condition for attracting people. «اتّبعوا من لا یسئلكم أجراً» The callers to truth (Haqq) should not ask people for a reward.

9- The guides (Hadiyan) must themselves be guided (Muhtadi). «اتّبعوا... مهتدون»

(605) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 201.

Ayahs

وَمَا لِىَ لَآ أَعْبُدُ ٱلَّذِى فَطَرَنِى وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۲۲)
Why should I not worship Him who has originated me, and to whom you shall be brought back? (22)
ءَأَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةً إِن يُرِدْنِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ بِضُرٍّ لَّا تُغْنِ عَنِّى شَفَـٰعَتُهُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يُنقِذُونِ (۲۳)
Shall I take gods besides Him? If the All-beneficent desired to cause me any distress, their intercession will not avail me in any way, nor will they rescue me. (23)
إِنِّىٓ إِذًا لَّفِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۲٤)
Indeed, then I would be in manifest error. (24)
إِنِّىٓ ءَامَنتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ فَٱسْمَعُونِ (۲٥)
Indeed I have faith in your Lord, so listen to me.’ (25)
قِيلَ ٱدْخُلِ ٱلْجَنَّةَ ۖ قَالَ يَـٰلَيْتَ قَوْمِى يَعْلَمُونَ (۲٦)
He was told, ‘Enter paradise!’ He said, ‘Alas! Had my people only known (26)
بِمَا غَفَرَ لِى رَبِّى وَجَعَلَنِى مِنَ ٱلْمُكْرَمِينَ (۲۷)
for what my Lord forgave me and made me one of the honoured ones!’ (27)

Key Points

Based on what is mentioned in Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, the incident relates to the time of Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام), when He sent two of His disciples (Hawariyyun) to the city of Antioch (Antakya) to invite the people, and on the way, they met Habib al-Najjar, and he believed due to a miracle shown by the two of them. The two entered the city, but the people did not accept their words and denied them. Prophet Christ (عليه السلام) sent "Sham'un" (Simon), his greatest disciple, to help them, and he was able to win the king's heart and gain his support, and most of the people believed in Allah. However, this narration, which is also mentioned in other exegetical books, is inconsistent with the text of the Qur'an in two respects: Firstly, the apparent meaning of the Quranic verses indicates the sending of a Prophet by Allah to this people, not merely the companions of a Prophet, and secondly, if the majority of the people had believed, the punishment would not have descended upon them.(606) Based on narrations, the individual who came to the city of Antioch to support the divine messengers was Habib al-Najjar, who is also referred to as the "Companion of Yasin" (Sahib Yasin) and is counted alongside the Believer from the family of Pharaoh who rose to support Prophet Moses (عليه السلام).(607) Today, the city of Antioch, which is located between Aleppo and Alexandria and is part of Turkish territory, is the second most important city for Christians after "Bayt al-Maqdis" (Jerusalem).

606) Tafsir Namuneh. 607) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Those who do not worship their Creator are condemned in the court of conscience. «و مالى لا اعبد»
  2. One way to invite others is by stating one's own logical beliefs. «مالى لا اعبد الّذى...»
  3. Attention to the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad) is the source of servitude (worship). «فطرنى و الیه ترجعون»
  4. Comparison is one of the best methods of teaching. Allah created me, but idols are not beneficial to me in the slightest. «فطرنى - لا تغن عنّى شفاعتهم»
  5. The hardships that a person faces are by the Will of Allah and within the sphere of His Mercy. «اِن یردن الرّحمن بضُرّ»
  6. To pay attention to Allah, it is sufficient to visualize difficult and extraordinary circumstances before ourselves. «اِن یردن الرّحمن بضُرّ»
  7. To avoid hurting people's feelings, we should use ourselves as an example. «اِن یردن الرّحمن بضُرّ...»
  8. The best method of invitation is practical invitation (action). «انّى آمنت»
  9. Martyrdom in support of a divine leader and enjoining the good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) has a very long history. «قیل ادخل الجنّة»
  10. There is no distance between martyrdom and Paradise. «قیل ادخل الجنّة»
  11. Men of Allah think of the living even after death and martyrdom. «یا لیت قومى یعلمون»
  12. The life and Paradise of Barzakh (the intermediate realm) precede the Promised Paradise. «قیل ادخل الجنّة»
  13. Martyrdom is a cause for forgiveness (Maghfirah). «بما غفر لى»

Ayahs

۞ وَمَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ مِن جُندٍ مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا كُنَّا مُنزِلِينَ (۲۸)
After him We did not send down on his people a host from the sky, nor We would have sent down. (28)
إِن كَانَتْ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَٰحِدَةً فَإِذَا هُمْ خَـٰمِدُونَ (۲۹)
It was but a single Cry, and, behold, they were stilled [like burnt ashes]! (29)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the story was narrated of a man who rose up alone to support the Divine Prophets, cried out (the truth), and was martyred in this path. This verse is meant to describe the destruction of the stubborn disbelievers who denied the Prophets and killed this man of God. Khāmid (خامد) is derived from khumūd (خمود), meaning the extinguishing of a flame, and here it refers to destruction and death.

Messages

1- Angels are the agents of Divine wrath or mercy, and their descent or non-descent is by the command of Allah. «ما انزلنا... جند من السماء»

2- Divine wrath is sudden; let us not be heedless. «صیحة واحدة»

3- Sometimes, due to the shedding of the blood of an innocent believer, a mujahid (striver), and a religious preacher, the people of an entire region are destroyed. «هم خامدون»

4- The superpowers who yesterday were saying: «لنرجمنكم و لیمسّنكم منا عذاب الیم» were destroyed today by a single blast. «هم خامدون»

Ayah

يَـٰحَسْرَةً عَلَى ٱلْعِبَادِ ۚ مَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۳٠)
How regrettable of the servants! There did not come to them any apostle but that they used to deride him. (30)

Messages

1- How does the human being, who can be a proclaimer of truth by taking a righteous stance and become blessed by following the prophets—as stated in "جعلنى من المكرمین"—subject himself to wrath through his obstinacy? "یا حسرة..."

2- Obstinacy is the blinding of the grounds for growth and proximity (to Allah) and the prelude to regrets. "یا حسرةً"

3- Awareness of the bitter events of history protects man. "یا حسرة... ما یأتیهم"

4- Mocking the prophets has been the constant method of the disbelievers throughout history. "كانوا به یستهزؤون"

5- All prophets were subjected to mockery. "من رسولٍ" (Therefore, religious preachers should expect the verbal abuse of others.)

Ayahs

أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ أَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ (۳۱)
Have they not regarded how many generations We have destroyed before them who will not come back to them? (31)
وَإِن كُلٌّ لَّمَّا جَمِيعٌ لَّدَيْنَا مُحْضَرُونَ (۳۲)
And all of them will indeed be presented before Us. (32)

Key Points

The word "qarn" (قَرن), according to Tafsir Namuneh, refers both to a long period of time and to the people who live during one time (a generation).

Messages

  1. Those who do not research history and do not take lessons from the fate of past generations are subject to criticism. "أ لَم یروْا" (Have they not seen?)

  2. Divine traditions (Sunan) in history are constant, and fates resemble one another; witnessing one scene can serve as a model for other scenes. "أ لَم یروْا..."

  3. Narrating history serves as a threat to criminals and a consolation to those who follow the path of truth. "ألَم یروْا كم اهلكنا"

  4. The result of mocking the Prophets is destruction. "كانوا به یستهزؤون... اهلكنا"

  5. After divine wrath, there is no way back or opportunity for compensation; let us not lose opportunities. "انّهم الیهم لا یرجعون"

  6. The destruction of the disbelievers is not the end of their affair, but rather the beginning of their torment. "لدینا محضرون" (They will be brought before Us).

  7. All people will be gathered in one place on the Day of Judgment. "كلٌّ... جمیع لدینا محضرون"

Ayah

وَءَايَةٌ لَّهُمُ ٱلْأَرْضُ ٱلْمَيْتَةُ أَحْيَيْنَـٰهَا وَأَخْرَجْنَا مِنْهَا حَبًّا فَمِنْهُ يَأْكُلُونَ (۳۳)
A sign for them is the dead earth, which We revive and bring forth grain out of it, so they eat of it. (33)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that all people will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment and presented before Allah; this Ayah explains the proof for the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and the revival of the dead.

Messages

1- Every seed and plant that grows from the earth is like a dead person rising from the grave on the Day of Judgment. «و آیة لهم»

2- To prove the truthfulness of our speech, we should argue using examples. «و آیة لهم»

3- The best proof for the general public is one that is permanent, universal, undeniable, simple, and ubiquitous. «و آیة لهم الارض»

4- The best way to believe in the Resurrection is to reflect upon creation. «الارض المیتة احییناها»

5- A major portion of human food consists of plant seeds, and reflection upon them is a path toward knowing Allah. «و آیة.... حبّاً فمنه تأكلون»

Ayahs

وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا جَنَّـٰتٍ مِّن نَّخِيلٍ وَأَعْنَـٰبٍ وَفَجَّرْنَا فِيهَا مِنَ ٱلْعُيُونِ (۳٤)
We make in it orchards of date palms and vines, and We cause springs to gush forth in it, (34)
لِيَأْكُلُوا۟ مِن ثَمَرِهِۦ وَمَا عَمِلَتْهُ أَيْدِيهِمْ ۖ أَفَلَا يَشْكُرُونَ (۳٥)
so that they may eat of its fruit and what their hands have cultivated. Will they not then give thanks? (35)

Key Points

In the Quran, among the fruits, the names of grapes and dates are mentioned most frequently. This is either due to the nutritional role of these two fruits, their accessibility, the diversity of their species, the utilization of both their dry and fresh forms, or other reasons. The two words, "نخیل" (date palms) and "اعناب" (grapes), are used in the plural form because both dates and grapes possess many varieties.

Messages

1- In nutrition, the primary role belongs to grains and seeds, and then to fruits. «اخرجنا منها حَبّاً... جعلنا فیها جنّات»

2- The duty of man regarding blessings is recognition (Ma'rifah) and gratitude (Shukr). (Allah revives the dead earth and makes plants and fruits accessible so that man may eat and be grateful.) «لیأكلوا من ثمره... أفلا یشكرون»

3- The production of various products from fruits is accepted by the Quran. «و ما عملته ایدیهم»

4- Stating the blessings causes the spirit of gratitude to arise in man. «أفلا یشكرون»

Ayah

سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلْأَزْوَٰجَ كُلَّهَا مِمَّا تُنۢبِتُ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَمِنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَمِمَّا لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳٦)
Immaculate is He who has created all the kinds of what the earth grows, and of themselves, and of what they do not know. (36)

Key Points

One of the scientific miracles of the Quran is its declaration of the law of pairing (duality) in all things: plants, humans, and that which people did not know at that time but know today. During the era of the Quran's revelation, people were familiar with pairing in the date palm, but today it has been established that the principle of pairing is universal throughout the realm of plants.

Messages

1- Our gratitude is a sign of our maturity; He has no need for our thanks and praise. «أفلا یشكرون سبحان الّذى»

2- Do not extend the law of pairing found in creation to the Divine Essence; He is Unique and peerless. «سبحان الّذى خلق الازواج»

3- His acts of creation and the created beings are signs of His power, will, and wisdom, not signs of His need or dependence. «سبحان الّذى خلق الازواج»

4- The emergence of seeds and fruits occurs through the law of pairing. «من ثمره... خلق الازواج كلها»

5- In physical and sexual matters, man is ranked alongside other creatures. «مما تنبت الارض و من انفسهم»

6- There are beings in existence whose manner of pairing is unknown to mankind. «خلق الازواج كلّها... مما لا یعلمون»

Ayahs

وَءَايَةٌ لَّهُمُ ٱلَّيْلُ نَسْلَخُ مِنْهُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فَإِذَا هُم مُّظْلِمُونَ (۳۷)
A sign for them is the night, which We strip of daylight, and, behold, they find themselves in the dark! (37)
وَٱلشَّمْسُ تَجْرِى لِمُسْتَقَرٍّ لَّهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ (۳۸)
The sun runs on to its place of rest: That is the ordaining of the All-mighty, the All-knowing. (38)
وَٱلْقَمَرَ قَدَّرْنَـٰهُ مَنَازِلَ حَتَّىٰ عَادَ كَٱلْعُرْجُونِ ٱلْقَدِيمِ (۳۹)
As for the moon, We have ordained its phases, until it becomes like an old palm leaf. (39)
لَا ٱلشَّمْسُ يَنۢبَغِى لَهَآ أَن تُدْرِكَ ٱلْقَمَرَ وَلَا ٱلَّيْلُ سَابِقُ ٱلنَّهَارِ ۚ وَكُلٌّ فِى فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ (٤٠)
Neither it behooves the sun to overtake the moon, nor may the night outrun the day, and each swims in an orbit. (40)

Key Points

'Urjūn' refers to the part of the date cluster that is attached to the tree and, after time passes, takes the shape of an arc (or crescent). The argument from design (Burhān al-Naẓm) is derived from these verses, which is one of the proofs for the existence of Allah. Drawing people's attention to the movement of the sun and the moon, the appearance of night and day, the changes observed in the moon, the floating of celestial bodies in the sky, the orbits of the celestial bodies, the lack of friction between the celestial bodies, and the precise, knowledgeable, and wise measurements, indicates Islam's attention to astronomy ('Ilm al-Hay'ah) and its creation of a suitable environment for the research of scientists throughout history.

Messages

1- Night is among the signs of Divine power and wisdom. «آیة لهم الیل»

2- A constant program throughout history, without any disruption, is a sign of the existence of a Wise and All-Knowing Reckoner. «ذلك تقدیر العزیز العلیم»

3- The appearance of night and day is not accidental; it is the work of Allah. «نسلخ منه النهار»

4- The orbits of the sun and the moon are designed in such a way that they never collide with each other, and no disruption occurs in the creation of night and day. «لا الشمس ینبغى لها ان تدرك القمر»

5- Contrary to the theory that the sun is stationary, the Quran considers the sun to be in motion, specifically, directional motion. «تجرى لمستقرّ لها»

6- No one can disrupt the order governing existence. «تقدیر العزیز...»

7- The metaphors used in the Quran are timeless. The movement of the moon is likened to the movement of a date cluster. «عاد كالعرجون القدیم»

8- All celestial bodies are moving in their own orbits. «كلّ فى فلك یسبحون»

9- The movement of celestial bodies in their orbits is rapid. ("Yasbaḥūn" means rapid swimming/floating.)

Ayahs

وَءَايَةٌ لَّهُمْ أَنَّا حَمَلْنَا ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ ٱلْمَشْحُونِ (٤۱)
A sign for them is that We carried their progeny in the laden ship, (41)
وَخَلَقْنَا لَهُم مِّن مِّثْلِهِۦ مَا يَرْكَبُونَ (٤۲)
and We have created for them what is similar to it, which they ride. (42)
وَإِن نَّشَأْ نُغْرِقْهُمْ فَلَا صَرِيخَ لَهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنقَذُونَ (٤۳)
And if We like We drown them, whereat they have no one to call for help, nor are they rescued (43)
إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا وَمَتَـٰعًا إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (٤٤)
except by a mercy from Us and for an enjoyment until some time. (44)

Key Points

The utilization of ships and animals has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran as divine blessings, but in this Ayah, it is presented as a means for carrying descendants (children) so that humanity may perceive the divine blessing through both intellect and emotion. «حملناهم ذریّتهم» Even today, if oil tankers and ships carrying wheat and foodstuffs do not move across the oceans, human life becomes paralyzed. Furthermore, transportation by sea is the cheapest way to transfer bulky and heavy goods.

Messages

1- It is Allah Who has arranged the water in such a way that man can move upon it by building ships, and this is among the signs of His divine power. «و آیة... انّا حملنا ذرّیتهم فى الفلك»

2- The conveyance and means of transport, which are necessities of human life, are a special blessing and a sign of divine grace and providence. «خلقنا لهم من مثله ما یركبون»

3- The hand of Allah is open in wrath and anger. Let us not be arrogant. «اِن نشأ نغرقهم»

4- If Allah’s wrath descends, no one will be a helper (or rescuer). Therefore, we should not feel one hundred percent secure. «اِن نشأ نغرقهم فلا صریخ لهم»

5- Never consider your salvation to be solely due to material factors. «رحمةً منّا»

6- Everyone has an appointed term (of life), and our life is limited. «متاعاً الى حین»

Ayahs

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱتَّقُوا۟ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَمَا خَلْفَكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (٤٥)
And when they are told, ‘Beware of that which is before you and that which is behind you, so that you may receive [His] mercy…’ (45)
وَمَا تَأْتِيهِم مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ مِّنْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضِينَ (٤٦)
There does not come to them any sign from among the signs of their Lord but that they have been disregarding it. (46)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the divine blessings were mentioned, which, if they pay attention, are all signs of His power, wisdom, and grace. In these verses, He says: Their obstinacy is such that they neither learn from the signs nor listen to the admonitions when they are told: Refrain from committing deeds that bring worldly punishment and the torment of the Hereafter, so that Allah may forgive your past and bestow His grace upon you.

Messages

  1. Complete the argument (Hujjah) against individuals through enjoining the good. «قیل لهم اتّقوا»
  2. Piety (Taqwa) is the key to receiving divine mercy. «اتّقوا... لعلّكم ترحمون»
  3. To complete the argument, present various types of evidence. «آیة من آیات ربّهم»
  4. The signs of Allah are numerous, but the acceptance by the people is scarce. «آیات ربّهم... عنها معرضون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَنفِقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَنُطْعِمُ مَن لَّوْ يَشَآءُ ٱللَّهُ أَطْعَمَهُۥٓ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (٤۷)
When they are told, ‘Spend out of what Allah has provided you,’ the faithless say to the faithful, ‘Shall we feed [someone] whom Allah would feed, if He wished? You are only in manifest error.’ (47)

Key Points

Individuals who lack correct insight or wish to shirk responsibility blame others for all their deviations and ugly actions: 1. Sometimes they say: Allah is to blame; if He had not willed it, we would not have been polytheists. «لو شاء اللّه ما اشركنا»(608), «لو شاء الرّحمن ما عبدناهم»(609) 2. Sometimes they say: Society is to blame; if these leaders had not existed, we would have been believers. «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین»(610) 3. Sometimes they say: Our ancestors are to blame. «انّا وجدنا آبائنا»(611) In this Ayah, too, they say: If it were necessary, Allah Himself would have provided sustenance to the poor. They are heedless of the fact that Allah has placed the responsibility of feeding the poor upon the wealthy. Question: Why does Allah not provide sustenance to the poor Himself, and instead command people to spend (in charity)? Answer: Human growth occurs under the shadow of forgiveness, sacrifice, generosity, philanthropy, compassion, thrift, affection, cooperation, love, and care. If all people enjoyed equal resources, there would remain neither the opportunity for these perfections to manifest nor the context for the patience, asceticism, and contentment of the deprived.

608) An'am, 148. 609) Zukhruf, 20. 610) Saba, 31. 611) Zukhruf, 22.

Messages

  1. Paying attention to the fact that "what we possess is Divine sustenance, not our property" makes giving easy. «انفقوا ممّا رزقكم اللّه»

  2. Disbelief (Kufr) is the cause of withholding charity. «قال الّذین كفروا... انطعم»

  3. The disbeliever mocks Allah's attribute as the Provider. «لو یشاء اللّه اطعمه»

  4. Spending (in charity) is a sign of faith. (Because abandoning charity is counted among the characteristics of the disbelievers.) «قال الّذین كفروا... انطعم...»

  5. Man reaches a point where he considers disbelief and stinginess to be the correct path, and faith and charity to be deviation. «اِن انتم الا فى ضلال مبین»

Ayahs

وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٤۸)
And they say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, should you be truthful?’ (48)
مَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَٰحِدَةً تَأْخُذُهُمْ وَهُمْ يَخِصِّمُونَ (٤۹)
They do not await but a single Cry that will seize them as they wrangle. (49)
فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ تَوْصِيَةً وَلَآ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (٥٠)
Then they will not be able to make any will, nor will they return to their folks. (50)

Messages

1- The disbelievers have no proof for denying the Resurrection (Qiyamah); they merely mock it by posing the question of when the Resurrection will occur. «متى هذا الوعد اِن كنتم صادقین»

2- The lack of a specified time for an event is not proof that the event will not happen. «متى هذا الوعد»

3- The Resurrection is a Divine promise. «هذا الوعد»

4- The disbelievers consider the prophets and the believers to be liars. «اِن كنتم صادقین»

5- The Resurrection gives no warning; why all this negligence and mockery? «صیحة واحدة»

6- When the Resurrection comes, the tongue will not have the power to speak, nor the feet the ability to flee. «فلا یستطیعون توصیة و لا... یرجعون»

7- The establishment of the Resurrection will follow one deadly blast. «صیحة واحدة»

8- The end of the world and the beginning of the Resurrection will occur while people are preoccupied with daily disputes and conflicts. «تأخذهم و هم یخصّمون»

9- Attachments and affections are effective in this world. With the advent of the Resurrection, all bonds will be severed. «و لا الى اهلهم یرجعون»

Ayahs

وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَإِذَا هُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَنسِلُونَ (٥۱)
And when the Trumpet is blown, behold, there they will be, scrambling towards their Lord from their graves! (51)
قَالُوا۟ يَـٰوَيْلَنَا مَنۢ بَعَثَنَا مِن مَّرْقَدِنَا ۜ ۗ هَـٰذَا مَا وَعَدَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ وَصَدَقَ ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ (٥۲)
They will say, ‘Woe to us! Who raised us from our place of sleep?’ ‘This is what the All-beneficent had promised, and the apostles had spoken the truth!’ (52)
إِن كَانَتْ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَٰحِدَةً فَإِذَا هُمْ جَمِيعٌ لَّدَيْنَا مُحْضَرُونَ (٥۳)
It will be but a single Cry, and, behold, they will all be presented before Us! (53)

Key Points

In the Quran, blowing the Trumpet (Sūr) is mentioned twice: once for the annihilation of the world, and the other for the establishment of the Day of Judgment (Qiyāmah). The intent of the blowing of the Trumpet (Nafkh fis-Sūr) in this Ayah is the second blowing of the Trumpet and the resurrection of the dead. Ajdhāth (أَجْدَاث) is the plural of Jadath (جَدَث), meaning grave. Yansilūn (يَنْسِلُونَ) is derived from Naslān, meaning to move quickly, and the word Marqad (مَرْقَد) is a noun of place derived from Ruqād, meaning place of rest, and its intent here is the grave. Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: The phrase, «هذا ما وعد الرّحمن و صدق المرسلون» (This is what the Most Compassionate promised, and the Messengers spoke the truth), is the speech of the angels to the people of the Day of Judgment.(612)

(612) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The beginning of the Day of Judgment is with a sound heard from the blowing of the Trumpet. «نفخ فى الصور فاذا هم من الاجداث»

2- The Resurrection (Ma'ād) is physical, and bodies will emerge from these very graves. «من الاجداث... ینسلون... بعثنا من مرقدنا»

3- The resurrection of humans on the Day of Judgment will be very swift and easy. «نفخ فاذا هم...»

4- The hurried movement is a sign of the anxiety and excitement of the people on the Day of Judgment. «ینسلون»

5- The Day of Judgment is a day of regret for the disbelievers. «یا ویلنا»

6- The Day of Judgment is a scene of awakening, affirmation, and confession. «هذا ما وَعد الرّحمن و صدق المرسلون»

7- The establishment of the Day of Judgment and accountability is a necessity of Allah being Ar-Rahmān (the Most Compassionate). «هذا ما وَعد الرّحمن»

8- On the Day of Judgment, all people will be gathered in one place. «جمیع لدینا محضرون»

9- Presence on the Day of Judgment is not voluntary. «مُحضرون»

Ayah

فَٱلْيَوْمَ لَا تُظْلَمُ نَفْسٌ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (٥٤)
‘Today no soul will be wronged in the least, nor will you be requited except for what you used to do.’ (54)

Key Points

This Ayah indicates the embodiment of deeds (Tajasum al-'Amal) on the Day of Judgment, because He says: Your recompense is nothing but your own deed. «ما كنتم تعملون» (what you used to do). However, if He had said: «بما كنتم تعملون», it would have meant that you are punished or rewarded because of the deeds you performed.

Messages

1- Allah is Just, and the Day of Judgment is the day of the manifestation and display of Divine Justice. «فالیوم لا تظلم نفسٌ» (Even the severest punishments are just). «لا تظلم نفس»

2- Punishment and reward on the Day of Judgment are based on our performance in this world. «ما كنتم تعملون»

Ayahs

إِنَّ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ٱلْيَوْمَ فِى شُغُلٍ فَـٰكِهُونَ (٥٥)
Indeed today the inhabitants of paradise rejoice in their engagements (55)
هُمْ وَأَزْوَٰجُهُمْ فِى ظِلَـٰلٍ عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ مُتَّكِـُٔونَ (٥٦)
—they and their mates, reclining on couches in the shades. (56)
لَهُمْ فِيهَا فَـٰكِهَةٌ وَلَهُم مَّا يَدَّعُونَ (٥۷)
There they have fruits, and they have whatever they want. (57)
سَلَـٰمٌ قَوْلًا مِّن رَّبٍّ رَّحِيمٍ (٥۸)
‘Peace!’—a watchword from the all-merciful Lord. (58)

Key Points

Shughul (شُغُل) means something that keeps a person occupied. Fākihūn (فاكهون) is derived from fakāhah (فكاهة), meaning joyful speech. Arā'ik (ارائك) is the plural of arīkah (اریكة), meaning an adorned couch or throne in the bridal chamber.

In Paradise, there is peace (salām) from every direction; Allah greets them: «سلام قولا من ربّ رحیم» (Peace—a word from a Merciful Lord). The angels greet them: «والملائكة یدخلون علیهم من كلّ باب سلام علیكم»(613) The inhabitants of Paradise also greet one another. «تحیّتهم فیها سلام»(614)

613) Ra'd, 24. 614) Yunus, 10.

Messages

1- In Paradise, there is no idleness. «فى شغل» (The word shughul (شُغُلٍ), which appears with tanwīn and is indefinite, signifies an occupation whose essence and depth are unknown and indescribable.)

2- In Paradise, there is no grief or sorrow. «فاكهون»

3- In Paradise, there is life without death, health without sickness, youth without old age, honor without humiliation, blessing without hardship, permanence without annihilation, contentment without anger, and intimacy without fear. «فى شغل فاكهون»

4- In Paradise, there is no loneliness. «هم و ازواجهم»

5- The dwelling in Paradise provides tranquility. «فى ظلال على الارائك»

6- Sitting on a couch or throne is superior to sitting on the ground. «على الارائك متّكئون»

7- In Paradise, there is the best sustenance. «لهم فیها فاكهة» (Fākihah (فاكهة) is indefinite, indicating that they will be served with extraordinary fruits.)

8- There are no limitations in Paradise. «و لهم ما یدّعون» (Yadda'ūn (یدعون) means that whatever you wish for, ask and desire it.)

9- The Resurrection is physical. (The words fruit, spouse, and couch indicate that we will live in the Hereafter with this very body.)

10- In Paradise, a magnificent contentment and greeting (salām) are received from Allah. «سلامٌ قولاً...»

11- Receiving the Divine greeting (salām) is the wish and desire of the inhabitants of Paradise. «و لهم ما یدعون سلامٌ...»

Ayahs

وَٱمْتَـٰزُوا۟ ٱلْيَوْمَ أَيُّهَا ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (٥۹)
And ‘Get apart today, you guilty ones!’ (59)
۞ أَلَمْ أَعْهَدْ إِلَيْكُمْ يَـٰبَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ أَن لَّا تَعْبُدُوا۟ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ (٦٠)
‘Did I not exhort you, O children of Adam, saying, “Do not worship Satan. He is indeed your manifest enemy. (60)
وَأَنِ ٱعْبُدُونِى ۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَٰطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ (٦۱)
Worship Me. That is a straight path”? (61)
وَلَقَدْ أَضَلَّ مِنكُمْ جِبِلًّا كَثِيرًا ۖ أَفَلَمْ تَكُونُوا۟ تَعْقِلُونَ (٦۲)
Certainly, he has led astray many of your generations. Have you not exercised your reason? (62)

Key Points

The separation of sinners from the righteous is according to the law of Divine Justice, as He said: the believer and the disbeliever are not equal: «أفمن كان مؤمنا كمن كان فاسقاً لا یستوون»(615) Perhaps the meaning of separation is the isolation of the criminals from one another, their becoming lonely, and their suffering the pain of separation, similar to solitary confinement in this world which separates criminals from each other. «جِبّلاً كثیراً» refers to a large multitude that possesses greatness like a mountain.(616) The intent behind worshipping Satan is obeying Satan, just as we read in the tradition (Hadith): «مَن اطاع رجلاً فى معصیة فقد عَبَده»(617) Whoever obeys a person in sin has worshipped him.

615) Surah As-Sajdah, 18. 616) Mufradat Raghib. 617) Wasā'il, Vol. 18, p. 79.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is the day of decision and separation. «وامتازوا الیوم»

2- Allah has taken a covenant from humanity (through the prophets, intellect, and innate nature). «ألم اعهد الیكم»

3- Satan deceived your father, and you are the children of one who obeyed Satan. «یا بنى آدم... لا تعبدوا الشیطان»

4- Humanity is not outside of two states: either a servant of Allah or a servant of Satan. «لا تعبدوا الشیطان... اعبدونى»

5- Satan's enmity is manifest. (There is no ambiguity in the deviation caused by a thought that presents stones and wood to man in place of Allah.) «عدوّ مبین»

6- The Straight Path, which we ask Allah for every day and night in prayer (Salat), is obedience to Him and worship of Him. «اُعبدونى هذا صراط مستقیم»

7- Take heed from the fate of those who were deceived. «اضلّ... جِبّلاً كثیراً انّكم تكونوا تعقلون»

8- Reflecting upon the bitter fate of the victims of Satan's deception is the path to salvation from his temptations and traps. «أفلا تكونوا تعقلون»

Ayahs

هَـٰذِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ ٱلَّتِى كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ (٦۳)
This is the hell you had been promised! (63)
ٱصْلَوْهَا ٱلْيَوْمَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (٦٤)
Enter it today, because of what you used to deny. (64)

Key Points

The word «اصلوها» (Burn therein) is derived from «صلى» (ṣalā), meaning to kindle a fire, to enter the fire, and to be inseparable from it.

Messages

1- Allah promised Hell many times, but the disbelievers did not believe. «توعدون - تكفرون» (you were promised - you disbelieved).

2- Allah has completed the argument (proof) against the inhabitants of Hell and has continuously informed them of the danger of the Fire. «كنتم توعدون» (you were being promised).

3- The entry of the disbelievers into Hell is accompanied by humiliation. (The command «اِصلوها» (Burn therein) is for contempt and insult.)

4- The disbelievers and the inhabitants of Hell will never be separated from the Fire. «اصلوها الیوم» (Burn therein today).

Ayah

ٱلْيَوْمَ نَخْتِمُ عَلَىٰٓ أَفْوَٰهِهِمْ وَتُكَلِّمُنَآ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَتَشْهَدُ أَرْجُلُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (٦٥)
Today We shall seal their mouths, and their hands shall speak to Us, and their feet shall bear witness concerning what they used to earn.’ (65)

Key Points

Mentioning the hands and feet is by way of example, because in other verses we read that the ears, eyes, and hearts will also be questioned (618), and even the skin will bear witness (619). In any case, every limb bears witness to what it has done.

(618) Isra, 36. (619) Sajdah, 20.

Messages

1- Allah nullifies means and creates means. He seals the mouth, which is the means of speech, and makes the hands and feet the means of speaking. «نختم على افواههم و تكلّمنا ایدیهم و... ارجلهم»

2- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical (corporeal). «تكلّمنا ایدیهم»

3- On the Day of Judgment, the limbs of the body are not subject to human will. «تكلّمنا ایدیهم و تشهد ارجلهم»

4- The strongest witness in court is the confession and admission of the perpetrator of the crime. «تشهد ارجلهم»

5- The limbs of the body possess a type of perception and consciousness that enables them to bear witness on the Day of Judgment. «تكلّمنا ایدیهم و تشهد ارجلهم»

Ayahs

وَلَوْ نَشَآءُ لَطَمَسْنَا عَلَىٰٓ أَعْيُنِهِمْ فَٱسْتَبَقُوا۟ ٱلصِّرَٰطَ فَأَنَّىٰ يُبْصِرُونَ (٦٦)
Had We wished We would have blotted out their eyes: then, were they to advance towards the path, how would have they seen? (66)
وَلَوْ نَشَآءُ لَمَسَخْنَـٰهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِهِمْ فَمَا ٱسْتَطَـٰعُوا۟ مُضِيًّا وَلَا يَرْجِعُونَ (٦۷)
And had We wished We would have deformed them in their place; then they would neither have been able to move ahead nor to return. (67)

Key Points

If we consider these two [verses] as a continuation of the previous verses regarding the punishment of the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment, they indicate the inability of the disbelievers to search for the path to Paradise and their astonishment and confusion in the Plain of Resurrection. However, most commentators consider these two [verses] related to the worldly retribution of the disbelievers and regard it as a type of threat involving the loss of eyesight and the inability to find their way home or their place of business.(620)

620) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

  1. Do not think that what you possess you must possess or will possess eternally. Do not be heedless of Allah's wrath and the change of blessings. «لو نشاء طمسنا»

  2. The remedy for an event must be sought before it occurs. One must abandon obstinacy before the descent of punishment. «لو نشاء... لو نشاء...»

  3. Every place can be the vessel for Allah's wrath. «على مكانتهم»

  4. When Allah's wrath arrives, no one has the power to endure it. «فما استطاعوا...»

  5. The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to leave humans free so that they may believe through seeing and hearing the truths, not that they should believe through wrath, compulsion, or pressure. «لو نشاء لمسخناهم» (The particle "لَو" (if) indicates that the tradition of Allah is not to transform people or immobilize them.)

Ayah

وَمَن نُّعَمِّرْهُ نُنَكِّسْهُ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ ۖ أَفَلَا يَعْقِلُونَ (٦۸)
And whomever We give a long life, We cause him to regress in creation. Then, will they not exercise their reason? (68)

Key Points

In the previous verses, Allah said: If We wish, We make the eye blind and change the faces; this verse explains an example of this change regarding the elderly. "ننكّسه" (nuknassuhu) is derived from "تنكیس" (tankees), meaning to invert or turn upside down, and what is meant by it is the return of man to the state of childhood. His knowledge turns into ignorance and forgetfulness, His power moves toward weakness, and His tolerance reaches irritability; His sensitivity increases, and His tears flow easily.

Messages

1- Life span is in the hand of Allah. "نعمّره"

2- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah that old age is accompanied by decline. "نعمّره ننكّسه" Although the effects of long life are in the hand of Allah. Where Allah wills, individuals like Noah and Mahdi علیهما السلام live long lives and do not decline.

4- Man must think about both the loss of blessings. "طمسنا", "مسخنا" and the weakening of them. "ننكّسه فى الخلق أفلا یعقلون"

Ayah

وَمَا عَلَّمْنَـٰهُ ٱلشِّعْرَ وَمَا يَنۢبَغِى لَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ وَقُرْءَانٌ مُّبِينٌ (٦۹)
We did not teach him poetry, nor does it behoove him. This is just a reminder and a manifest Quran, (69)

Key Points

One of the accusations leveled against the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, besides being called a sorcerer, madman, and soothsayer, was the accusation of being a poet, whereas:

Poetry arises from imagination, contrary to revelation. Poetry arises from emotions and feelings, contrary to revelation. Poetry is mixed with exaggeration, contrary to revelation.

Although the Quran possesses rhythmic and segmented sentences like poetry, the main element of poetry, which is imagination, has no place in the Quran. The Quran is nothing but a reminder (Dhikr). «اِن هو الاّ ذكر»

A reminder of Allah's power and wrath, a remembrance of His favors and blessings, a remembrance of His pardon and forgiveness, a remembrance of His traditions and laws, a remembrance of His Prophets, successors, and saints, a remembrance of history lessons full of admonition, a remembrance of the factors leading to the glory and downfall of nations, a remembrance of the righteous and the guided, a remembrance of the corrupt, the disbelievers, the wicked, the criminals, and the oppressors and their ultimate fate, a remembrance of sincerity, sacrifices, bravery, charity, patience, and their good end, a remembrance of murders, tortures, harassments, slanders, humiliations, denial of rights, and the subjugation of the weak, and the evil fate of the arrogant, a remembrance of commands, prohibitions, admonitions, and wisdoms, a remembrance of celestial, terrestrial, and marine creations, a remembrance of the future of history and the victory of the government of truth, the filling of the world with justice and equity, and the condemnation of tyranny and oppression, a remembrance of the Barzakh (intermediate realm), the Resurrection (Ma'ad), the events preceding the Day of Judgment, the nature of the scenes of Resurrection, the dangers of Hell, and the blessings of Paradise. Yes, the entire Quran is, in some way, a reminder and admonition. «اِن هو الاّ ذكر»

Messages

1- In the Quran, the discussion is not about negating the value of poetry, but about negating poetry from the Prophet. «و ما علّمناه الشعر»

2- Allah defends the Prophets against accusations. «و ما علّمناه الشعر»

3- Not knowing poetry does not harm the guidance of the preacher. «و ما علّمناه الشعر»

4- Improper developments and incorrect cultural environments should not destabilize you. (During the time of the Prophet, the issue of poetry held a status beyond the ordinary, and poetry and being a poet were paramount. This Ayah broke that grandeur.) «و ما علّمناه الشعر»

5- The nature of poetry (poetic talent) is from Allah. «و ما علّمناه الشعر»

6- The Teacher is the Prophet of God. «علّمناه»

7- Not every knowledge is appropriate for everyone. «و ما ینبغى له»

8- Unless we are emptied of poetic imaginations and delusions, our soul will not become a vessel for Divine knowledge. «و ما علّمناه الشعر... اِن هو الاّ ذكر و قرآن مبین»

9- The Quran is a source of admonition and remembrance. «اِن هو الاّ ذكر»

10- The Quran is a clear discourse, understandable and open to reasoning. «قرآن مبین»

Ayah

لِّيُنذِرَ مَن كَانَ حَيًّا وَيَحِقَّ ٱلْقَوْلُ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۷٠)
so that anyone who is alive may be warned, and that the word may come due against the faithless. (70)

Key Points

Life is of several types: 1. Vegetative life. «یحیى الارض بعد موتها»(621) Allah revives the earth (by causing plants to grow). 2. Animal life. «كنتم امواتا فاحیاكم»(622) You were dead, then He revived you. 3. Intellectual life. «دعاكم لما یحییكم»(623) The Prophet invites you to that which revives your (dead hearts). 4. Social life. «و لكم فى القصاص حیاة»(624) Your life (political and social, which is achieved under the shadow of security) lies in just retribution (Qisas) and vengeance. 5. Cardiac and spiritual life. «لیُنذر مَن كان حیّا» (the Ayah under discussion) The Quran warns individuals who possess living hearts and pure, ready souls. Yes, believers are people with living hearts, enjoying real and rational life, while disbelievers are like the dead, deprived of real life. Based on narrations, the meaning of «حیّاً» is the rational person.(625) The issue of Allah's inevitable wrath upon the disbelievers, which is mentioned in this Ayah with the phrase «یحقّ القول على الكافرین», is also raised in Surah Az-Zumar, Ayah 71: «و لكن حقّت كلمة العذاب على الكافرین» The warnings of the Quran originate from the source of Divine knowledge. The warnings of the Quran are diverse, well-known, and wise. The warnings of the Quran are accompanied by glad tidings and innovative methods. The warnings of the Quran are accompanied by tangible examples throughout time and space. The warner in this Ayah, «لینذر», can be the Quran, or it can be the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, who was mentioned in the previous Ayah.

621) Rum, 19. 622) Baqarah, 28. 623) Anfal, 24. 624) Baqarah, 179. 625) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The warnings of the Quran are a source of remembrance and awareness. «اِن هو الاّ ذكر... لینذر»

2- The sign of a living heart is the acceptance of the Quran's warnings. «لینذر من كان حیّا»

3- Guidance acceptance from the Quran is not exclusive to a specific nation or race. «لینذر مَن كان حیّاً»

4- The goal of revelation and the mission of the prophets is the awakening and alertness of those with living hearts, and the completion of the argument (Hujjah) against those with dead hearts. «و یحقّ القول على الكافرین»

5- Even the Word of Allah has no effect on the hard-hearted. «مَن كان حیّاً»

6- Disbelievers and those who do not accept the truth are nothing more than the dead. (In contrast to «مَن كان حیّاً», «الكافرین» is mentioned.)

7- The promise of Divine wrath for the disbelievers is the definitive Word of God. «یحقّ القول على الكافرین»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا خَلَقْنَا لَهُم مِّمَّا عَمِلَتْ أَيْدِينَآ أَنْعَـٰمًا فَهُمْ لَهَا مَـٰلِكُونَ (۷۱)
Have they not seen that We have created for them—of what Our hands have worked—cattle, so they have become their masters? (71)

Messages

1- Man can seek help in recognizing the power of Allah by paying attention to the creatures that are accessible to him. «أولم یروا...»

2- The creation of livestock (such as cows, sheep, camels, etc.) is for man. «خلقنا لهم »

3- In the production of plants and fruits, man had a role, «لیأكلوا من ثمره و ما عملته ایدیهم»(626) but in the creation of livestock, man has no role. «عملت ایدینا انعاماً»

4- The creation by Allah is original, not imitative. «عملت ایدینا»

5- Beings other than man also benefit from the sun, plants, and the sea, but only man utilizes livestock. «خلقنا لهم... فهم لها مالكون»

6- It is sufficient as a sign of Allah's grace that man plays no role in the emergence of animals, yet he easily becomes their owner. «ایدینا... فهم لها مالكون»

7- The principle of ownership is accepted by Islam. «فهم لها مالكون»(626) Yā Sīn, 35.

Ayahs

وَذَلَّلْنَـٰهَا لَهُمْ فَمِنْهَا رَكُوبُهُمْ وَمِنْهَا يَأْكُلُونَ (۷۲)
And We made them tractable for them; so some of them make their mounts and some of them they eat. (72)
وَلَهُمْ فِيهَا مَنَـٰفِعُ وَمَشَارِبُ ۖ أَفَلَا يَشْكُرُونَ (۷۳)
There are other benefits for them therein, and drinks. Will they not then give thanks? (73)

Messages

1- If cattle and sheep were wild, the world of dairy, with all its benefits, would be closed off to humanity. «ذلّلناها لهم... و منها یأكلون»

2- If all animals were wild, many journeys would not be possible. «ذلّلناها لهم فمنها ركوبهم»

3- Both the earth is submissive and manageable, «و الارض ذلولاً»(627) and the animals are submissive, «ذلّلناها». Yet, man, who is dependent on both, is rebellious. «انّ الانسان لیطغى »(628)

4- Everything has been created for a specific purpose. «منها ركوبهم و منها یأكلون»

5- Consuming food raw is not praised in Islam; rather, preparation is implied regarding the consumption of meat. «و منها یأكلون»

6- Milk is a special blessing for which gratitude must be shown. (Although milk is included among the general benefits of the animals, it is specifically mentioned as «مشارب» (drinks) to highlight its unique importance.) «و لهم فیها منافع و مشارب»

7- Gratitude must be based on understanding and knowledge. «أولم یَرو... أفلا یشكرون»

(627) Mulk, 15.

(628) Alaq, 6.

Ayahs

وَٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ءَالِهَةً لَّعَلَّهُمْ يُنصَرُونَ (۷٤)
They have taken gods besides Allah, [hoping] that they might be helped [by the fake deities]. (74)
لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُمْ جُندٌ مُّحْضَرُونَ (۷٥)
[But] they cannot help them, while they [themselves] are an army mobilized for their defence. (75)
فَلَا يَحْزُنكَ قَوْلُهُمْ ۘ إِنَّا نَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ (۷٦)
So do not let their remarks grieve you. We indeed know whatever they hide and whatever they disclose. (76)

Key Points

Human clothing comes from animals. From wool, the industry of carpets, blankets, and textile factories is established. Human shoes come from animal hides. For the use of animal hides, leather manufacturing factories are established, producing bags, shoes, hats, and more. Human food comes from the milk and meat of animals. "و لهم فیها منافع" (And for them [there are] benefits therein). The role of animals in agriculture is very important. Such as: plowing, drawing water, natural fertilizer, consuming weeds, carrying loads and produce, and threshing the harvest are among the benefits of animals for mankind. "فیها منافع و مشارب" (In them [are] benefits and drinks).

Messages

1- Polytheism and idol worship are acts of ingratitude for divine blessings. "أفلا یشكرون واتّخذوا من دون اللّه آلهةً"

2- The motivation of idolaters is based on fantasies and delusions. "واتّخذوا من دون اللّه آلهة لعلهم ینصرون"

3- When speaking with the misguided, target the source of their beliefs. "لا یستطیعون نصرهم"

4- The punishment for one who turns to idols instead of contemplating blessings and offering thanks to Allah is being summoned to enter Hellfire. "محضرون"

5- Prophets also need morale boosting, and Allah consoles them. "فلا یحزنك"

6- Anyone who has a lofty goal is subjected to various types of attacks. "لا یحزنك قولهم"

7- Attention to the knowledge and awareness of Allah is the best means of tranquility for the believer and a threat to the misguided. "نعلم"

8- Enemies plot conspiracies in secret and in public. "یسرّون، یعلنون"

Ayahs

أَوَلَمْ يَرَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ أَنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰهُ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ فَإِذَا هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُّبِينٌ (۷۷)
Does not man see that We created him from a drop of [seminal] fluid, and, behold, he is an open contender!? (77)
وَضَرَبَ لَنَا مَثَلًا وَنَسِىَ خَلْقَهُۥ ۖ قَالَ مَن يُحْىِ ٱلْعِظَـٰمَ وَهِىَ رَمِيمٌ (۷۸)
He draws comparisons for Us, and forgets his own creation. He says, ‘Who shall revive the bones when they have decayed?’ (78)
قُلْ يُحْيِيهَا ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَهَآ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۖ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ خَلْقٍ عَلِيمٌ (۷۹)
Say, ‘He will revive them who produced them the first time, and He has knowledge of all creation. (79)
ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلشَّجَرِ ٱلْأَخْضَرِ نَارًا فَإِذَآ أَنتُم مِّنْهُ تُوقِدُونَ (۸٠)
He, who made for you fire out of the green tree, and, behold, you light fire from it! (80)

Key Points

One of the polytheists brought a piece of decayed bone before the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, crumbled it onto the ground, and said: "Who will revive these decayed bones?" These verses were revealed in response to this doubt and question. (629) The tree intended in this verse refers to two types of fire-starting wood called Marakh and Afār, which the Arabs used to strike against each other to produce a spark, much like modern matches. (630) Producing fire from a green tree is an example that common people understand, and scholars also grasp its scientific method due to the energy stored within the tree. «من الشجر الاخضر نارا»

629) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 630) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- If man remembers his own weakness and insignificance, he will never act arrogantly. «أولم یر الانسان»

2- Attention to the creation of man from a drop of fluid (nutfah) strengthens faith in the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «من نطفة»

3- Worse than the mere existence of hostility is when the hostility is intense and manifest. «خصیمٌ مبین»

4- Man's contention with Allah is unexpected and astonishing. «فاذا هو خصیمٌ» (The word idha [إذا] is used for an unexpected event.)

5- Quoting and stating false ideas is permissible in order to respond to them. «ضرب لنا مثلاً»

6- The root of many objections regarding the power of Allah is the failure to pay attention to similar examples and the forgetting of past precedents. «و نسى خلقه»

7- Deniers of the Resurrection have no proof; all they have is incredulity (finding it remote or unlikely). «مَن یحیى العظام؟»

8- Freedom in Islam is such that deniers of God and the Resurrection speak their minds openly and with complete boldness before the leader of the Muslims. «مَن یحیى العظام»

9- Asking a question is not prohibited; what is bad are stubborn and arrogant motives. «مَن یحیى العظام»

10- The root of some doctrinal objections is the comparison between human power and the power of Allah. «مَن یحیى العظام»

11- Doctrinal doubts must be answered, even if the doubt is raised by only one person. «قال... قل»

12- The Resurrection is physical (bodily). «یحیى العظام»

13- The Resurrection requires two things: first, the power of Allah to recreate humans, and second, His knowledge of people's deeds. This verse refers to both: «أنشأها اوّل مرّة» is a sign of His power, and «هو بكلّ شى ء علیم» is a sign of His knowledge.

14- Allah combines opposites, such as water and fire, which are incompatible with each other. «من الشجر الاخضر نارا»

Ayah

أَوَلَيْسَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِقَـٰدِرٍ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يَخْلُقَ مِثْلَهُم ۚ بَلَىٰ وَهُوَ ٱلْخَلَّـٰقُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۸۱)
Is not He who created the heavens and the earth able to create the like of them? Yes indeed! He is the All-creator, the All-knowing. (81)

Key Points

Man is composed of body and soul. The human body disintegrates upon death and is constantly changing even during life, but the human soul and personality are fixed. In the Hereafter, the human soul will be the same worldly soul, but the human body will be in a mold similar to the mold of his worldly body. «یخلق مثلهم»

Messages

1- Sometimes, a question must be answered with a question. «مَن یحیى العظام... أولیس الّذى...»

2- Questioning is a way to awaken consciences. «أولیس الّذى...»

3- When speaking with deniers, we should start with small issues until we reach major issues. (The first example was the sperm drop, the next example was the green tree, and the third example was the creation of the heavens.) «خلق السموات»

4- Since the greater task is accomplished, the lesser task is also possible. He who created the heavens and the earth can also create human beings. «خلق السموات... بقادر»

5- His work is creation, whether in this world or in the Hereafter. «هو الخلاّق»

6- The proof of the Resurrection (Ma'ad), besides Divine wisdom and justice, is His power and knowledge over the deeds of human beings. «هو الخلاّق العلیم»

Ayahs

إِنَّمَآ أَمْرُهُۥٓ إِذَآ أَرَادَ شَيْـًٔا أَن يَقُولَ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ (۸۲)
All His command, when He wills something, is to say to it ‘Be,’ and it is. (82)
فَسُبْحَـٰنَ ٱلَّذِى بِيَدِهِۦ مَلَكُوتُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۸۳)
So immaculate is He in whose hand is the dominion of all things, and to whom you shall be brought back. (83)

Key Points

Since faith in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) must be very serious and strong, any removal of doubt concerning it must be carried out in such a way that not a speck of flaw remains in the mind. In response to the person who crushed a decayed bone and scattered it on the ground, Allah gave several answers, the last of which is this Ayah. "انّما امره اذا..." The realization of the Divine command and will does not require time; it is, figuratively speaking, like the blink of an eye. «و ما امرنا الاّ واحدة كلمح بالبصر»(631) Allah does not even need to utter the word "كُن" (Be), because this itself implies need, and it is not befitting His dignity. Therefore, the intent of the word "كُن" is His Will and Decree itself. According to the statement of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام: «لا بصوت یقرع و لابنداء یسمع»(632).

631) Qamar, 50. 632) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 186.

Messages

1- For Allah, the creation of all things is the same. «اذا اراد شیئاً»

2- In the creation of existence, Allah requires neither means, nor assistance, nor anyone to remove obstacles. «كن فیكون»

3- There is no interval between God's Will and the execution of the deed. «كن فیكون»

4- How can you deem God incapable of establishing the Resurrection? He is free from every weakness and inability. «فسبحان الّذى»

5- Both the source of existence is in His hand, and the return of everything is towards Him. «بیده ملكوت كلّ شى ء و الیه ترجعون»
